title: Direction D: EML-1 Class Characterization

partition_function_Z.normalization: NONE — Z itself is the unnormalized sum
partition_function_Z.why_eml1: Sum of exp terms; no log taken; not yet normalized to probability
partition_function_Z.contrast: F = -log(Z)/β is EML-2: here the log appears

ground_state_energy.why_eml1: Single exponential suppression; no log factor

bcs_gap.why_eml1: Exponential gap; the Cooper pair binding energy

birkhoff_convergence.normalization: NONE — rate of approach, not a normalized quantity

qec_threshold.why_eml1: Exponential suppression of error below threshold

decoherence_factor.why_eml1: Pure exponential decay; not yet integrated/logged

wigner_surmise.normalization: PARTIAL — but the characteristic feature is the exp factor
wigner_surmise.why_eml1: Level-spacing distribution: exp factor dominates EML depth

primitive_test.rule: EML-1 = exp without log; EML-2 = exp + log paired; EML-3 = exp(imaginary)
primitive_test.key_distinction: The log partner is what elevates EML-1 to EML-2

eml1_analysis.common_property: All EML-1 objects = single exp without normalization/log partner
eml1_analysis.key_insight: EML-1 = exp-only class; Z=EML-1 because log(Z) is taken to get F=EML-2

eml_depth_summary.EML-1_definition: exp without log partner = single exponential primitive
eml_depth_summary.contrast_eml2: EML-2 = exp + log paired (= integration = normalization)

key_theorem: The EML-1 Class Theorem (S231, Direction D): EML-1 = the single exponential class: objects of the form exp(f) where f is EML-0 or EML-1, and no outer logarithm is applied. The KEY distinction from EML-2: in EML-2, exp and log appear TOGETHER (paired as ∫ = exp-sum + log-normalization). In EML-1, exp appears ALONE — no normalization, no log partner. Partition function Z = EML-1 precisely because log(Z) hasn't been taken yet. The moment you write F = -log(Z)/β, you're at EML-2. The transition Z → F is the canonical EML-1 → EML-2 step: adding the log partner.
rabbit_hole_log: Z=EML-1, F=EML-2: the partition function is the clearest EML-1→2 example
EML-1 = exp alone; EML-2 = exp+log; the log partner is what creates the integration layer
All exponential decays/growths without normalization = EML-1
